title: Airavatesvara Temple
tagline: Airavatesvara Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Airavatesvara Temple is a Hindu temple of Chola architecture located in Darasuram, a suburb of Kumbakonam, Thanjavur District in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
overview.description: Airavatesvara Temple is a Hindu temple of Chola architecture located in Darasuram, a suburb of Kumbakonam, Thanjavur District in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This temple, built by Chola emperor Rajaraja II in the 12th century CE is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, along with the Brihadeeswara Temple at Thanjavur, the Gangaikondacholisvaram Temple at Gangaikonda Cholapuram that are referred to as the Great Living Chola Temples. howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Darasuram
topPlaces.description: Darasuram or Dharasuram is a neighbourhood in the city of Kumbakonam, Tamil Nadu, India. The area is known for the Airavateswara temple constructed by the Rajaraja Chola II in the 12th century CE. The temple is a recognised UNESCO World Heritage monument.

topPlaces.name: Kambatta Viswanathar Temple
topPlaces.description: Kambatta Viswanathar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located at Kumbakonam in Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Adi Kumbeswarar Temple, Kumbakonam
topPlaces.description: Adi Kumbeswarar Temple, Kumbakonam is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, located in the town of Kumbakonam in Thanjavur District Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshiped as Adi Kumbeswarar, and is represented by the lingam.

topPlaces.name: Kodeeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Kodeeswarar Temple (Tamil: : கொட்டையூர் கோடீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, located in Kottaiyur, a village on the western outskirts of Kumbakonam, in Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshipped as Koteeswarar and His consort Parvathi as Pandhadu Nayaki.

topPlaces.name: Ramaswamy Temple, Kumbakonam
topPlaces.description: Ramaswamy Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Rama, an incarnation of Vishnu located in Kumbakonam, Tamil Nadu, India. It is one of the prominent temples in the town and also one of the most prominent temples dedicated to Rama in India.

topPlaces.name: Valaiyapettai
topPlaces.description: Valaiyapettai is a village in the Kumbakonam taluk of Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Someswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Someswarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Kumbakonam, Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshipped as Someswarar and is represented by the lingam.

topPlaces.name: Pazhayarai Vadathali
topPlaces.description: Pazhaiyarai vadathali Temple, or Pazhaiyarai Vadathali is a Hindu temple dedicated to Someswarar, a form of Shiva. It is located 6 km from Kumbakonam, Tamilnadu, India, on the Kumbakonam- Aavoor Road, 2 km away from the Thenupuriswarar Temple at Patteeswaram and 3 km from Darasuram.

items.note: Darasuram or Dharasuram is a neighbourhood in the city of Kumbakonam, Tamil Nadu, India. The area is known for the Airavateswara temple constructed by the Rajaraja Chola II in the 12th century CE. The temple is a recognised UNESCO World Heritage monument.

Kodeeswarar Temple (Tamil: : கொட்டையூர் கோடீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, located in Kottaiyur, a village on the western outskirts of Kumbakonam, in Thanjavur district, Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshipped as Koteeswarar and His consort Parvathi as Pandhadu Nayaki.

Ramaswamy Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Rama, an incarnation of Vishnu located in Kumbakonam, Tamil Nadu, India. It is one of the prominent temples in the town and also one of the most prominent temples dedicated to Rama in India.

Someswarar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Kumbakonam, Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshipped as Someswarar and is represented by the lingam.

Pazhaiyarai vadathali Temple, or Pazhaiyarai Vadathali is a Hindu temple dedicated to Someswarar, a form of Shiva. It is located 6 km from Kumbakonam, Tamilnadu, India, on the Kumbakonam- Aavoor Road, 2 km away from the Thenupuriswarar Temple at Patteeswaram and 3 km from Darasuram.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Airavatesvara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Airavatesvara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Airavatesvara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (74 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (75 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Airavatesvara Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Airavatesvara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Airavatesvara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Airavatesvara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Airavatesvara Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Darasuram, Kambatta Viswanathar Temple, Adi Kumbeswarar Temple, Kumbakonam, Kodeeswarar Temple, Ramaswamy Temple, Kumbakonam.
